description: Glossary file parses and every entry has en-term, pt-br-term, and notes; no duplicate keys.
Forbidden-terms list is non-empty and contains the canonical European-PT markers (comboio, telemóvel, ecrã, autocarro, registo, utilizador, rato).
Audit script exits non-zero when a seeded forbidden term (e.g. "comboio") is present in any pt file and zero when clean.
Audit script correctly counts identical-to-English keys against the allowlist (known fixture namespace).
Review-export produces one row per pt key with en source, pt value, namespace, and review status.
validate-translations.cjs passes for pt common.json (keys present, no extras, interpolation + plural forms valid).
audit-pt-br.cjs reports 0 untranslated (identical-to-en minus allowlist) and 0 forbidden-term violations for common.json.

Language-picker helper (filterPseudoLocales) still omits pt while INCOMPLETE_LOCALES contains it.
localeNames.pt renders as 'Português (Brasil)' wherever locale names are shown.
Repo-wide validator + audit both exit 0 for pt; native-review export file is generated and non-empty.
Setting locale=pt via cookie/URL loads pt resources for a sampled MSP and client-portal route without falling back to English.
Each listed template file exports a pt variant with non-empty subject/html/text and identical {{variable}} set to en.
pt email copy for this group passes the glossary forbidden-term audit.
Each listed template file exports a pt variant with non-empty subject/html/text and identical {{variable}} set to en.
pt email copy for this group passes the glossary forbidden-term audit.
Each listed template file exports a pt variant with non-empty subject/html/text and identical {{variable}} set to en.
pt email copy for this group passes the glossary forbidden-term audit.
Migration up() inserts 36 pt rows into system_email_templates; re-running is a no-op; down() removes only language_code=pt rows.
After migrate, emailLocaleResolver returning pt selects the pt template (not English fallback) for a client-portal recipient.
Every pt email row has the same {{variable}} tokens as its English counterpart (no dropped/renamed placeholders).
Migration inserts one pt row per internal_notification subtype (parity with the en row count); re-run is a no-op.
getUserLocale resolving to pt returns the pt internal-notification template for both an internal and a client user; falls back to en only when a pt row is absent.
pt notification title/message pass the glossary forbidden-term audit and preserve all {{variable}} placeholders.
Parity check fails if any en template lacks a pt counterpart, and passes once all pt rows exist.
Variable-set diff is empty for all pt vs en template pairs (email + notifications).
